title: rosa villosa var. recondita
intro: Rosa villosa var. recondita, also known as the "Wood's Rose" or "Wood's Rose Thicket Rose", is a variety of the Rosa villosa species, a type of wild rose native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rosa villosa var. recondita include treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, as well as supporting cardiovascular health. Rosa villosa var. recondita can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
